name: Rico Dowdle
headline: Proven 1,000-yard grinder lands in Pittsburgh committee
body:
Dowdle has quietly been a back-to-back 1,000-yard rusher: 1,076 yards at 4.56 per carry with six scores in 2025 (177.3 points, RB14) after 1,079 yards (4.59 YPC, RB17) in 2024. The 2025 tape shows a complete back, with a 10.17 percent explosive-run rate, 14 broken tackles, 2.03 yards after contact and a tidy single fumble on 236 carries, plus pass-game utility on 39 catches for 297 yards and a 10.4 percent target share. The -0.008 EPA per play is essentially neutral on a heavy workload.

The undrafted South Carolina product (rookie 2020) now joins Pittsburgh, where the snag is a likely timeshare rather than the bell-cow role that fueled his Carolina numbers. If he wins the early-down and goal-line work, his three-down profile and 39 red-zone opportunities translate to RB2 output; if it splits, he settles as a flex. Draft him as a high-floor RB2/flex with locked-in efficiency but a workload that depends on beating out the depth chart.
